title: Review documentation for OSS readiness
description: Review top-level and pack documentation after recent changes, make targeted OSS-readiness updates, validate docs and artifact metadata, then commit and push the resulting workspace state.
acceptance_criteria: README and key documentation describe the suite accurately; contribution, install, artifact, and pack guidance are OSS-ready; model/artifact manifests remain valid; quality gates pass; changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Added OSS readiness docs and GitHub templates; validation passed with Go tests, artifact checks, and suite drift check.

title: Add visual source-first artifact doctrine
description: Extend skill-harness artifact governance beyond UML-first so product, business, data, research, UX, and mockup work use canonical agent-readable sources with generated visual human review surfaces and team-agent ownership rules.
close_reason: Implemented visual-source-first artifact doctrine, scaffold config, templates, agent loadout wiring, docs, and model updates; validation passed.

title: Fix model checker contract for default-on modeling
description: Update the model artifact policy checker and fixtures for default-on modeling: dependency and architecture-space handling, method-less/generated evidence cases, profile-sensitive HTML requirements, legacy loose model-view compatibility, stale hashes, UWE facets, and model-diff lineage.
close_reason: Implemented mode-aware model checker contract, evidence model method support, profile-sensitive HTML review requirements, and regression tests.

title: Add modeling modes and default UML-first fresh setup
description: Replace the boolean modeling flag with explicit modeling modes off/baseline/uml-first. Fresh developer-artifact setups should default to UML-first while preserving profile none and skip-artifact flows. Keep --enable-modeling as a compatibility alias and add an opt-out such as --skip-modeling or --modeling-mode off.
close_reason: Added modeling modes with auto/off/baseline/uml-first behavior, fresh UML-first defaults, legacy preservation, opt-out flags, proof metadata, docs, and tests.

title: Implement demo production media capability
description: Research video/demo tooling and implement the first skill-harness media capability slice: demo-production skills, profile guidance, docs, and wiring.
notes: Implemented media artifact profile and demo-production-skills first slice: embedded pack with six source-backed demo/QA/media skills, setup-project media scaffold, docs, loadout/dependency wiring, global skill install/render, and validation. Follow-ups: skill-harness-ywj install-proof artifact and skill-harness-auk integration harness.
close_reason: Implemented the media developer-artifact profile and demo-production-skills embedded pack, wired loadouts/dependencies/docs, installed/rendered affected agents globally, and verified with Go tests, skill validation, JSON checks, and skill-harness check.

title: Experiment 8: multi-session compounding (3 sequential feature additions)
description: Run 3 sequential agent sessions on the same project in both groups. Session 1: build auth module. Session 2: fresh agent adds task CRUD. Session 3: fresh agent adds rate limiting. Measures whether the toolkit's traceability compounds positively (each fresh agent benefits from prior specs) or becomes a burden (spec maintenance overhead). Group A maintains specgraph chain across all 3 sessions; Group B is raw Claude each time.

title: Experiment 5: component isolation (specgraph-only vs noslop-only vs full vs baseline)
description: Run auth module task with 4 groups: A=full toolkit (specgraph+noslop+AGENTS.md), B=specgraph-only (spec-first + annotations + verify, no noslop hooks), C=noslop-only (AGENTS.md with noslop gates only, no specgraph), D=baseline. Identifies which component drives the toolkit advantage. Hypothesis: specgraph drives spec/evidence metrics; noslop contribution is minimal (hooks not wired in test env); combination provides marginal benefit over specgraph-only.

title: Experiment 7: ambiguous requirements test
description: Give both groups a deliberately underspecified task: 'Build a notification system that sends alerts when things happen.' No further detail. Group A must use spec-writer to clarify and nail down requirements before coding. Group B just has to figure it out. Hypothesis: spec-first workflow forces clarification that prevents drift and over-engineering on vague tasks — this is where the toolkit's process value should show most clearly. Score with special attention to drift, over-engineering, and spec compliance.
close_reason: Closed

title: Experiment 4: maintenance/handoff test
description: Give a fresh agent the output from session 1A (annotated, spec-tracked) and session 1B (no specs) and ask each to add a new feature: 'add a password reset flow with a one-time token that expires after 15 minutes.' Score the fresh agent's output on the same 7 metrics. This tests whether the paper trail has actual utility for a subsequent agent — the core question of whether traceability is value or overhead.
close_reason: Closed

title: Experiment 3: larger project (task manager API)
description: Run the benchmark on a larger greenfield project: a task management REST API with CRUD endpoints, middleware, input validation, and integrated auth (reusing the auth module). Approx 300-500 LOC. Group A uses full toolkit (spec-first, annotations, verify). Group B raw Claude. Hypothesis: toolkit advantage extends beyond traceability to correctness/completeness on larger scope. Measure same 7 metrics.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add noslop-skills pack to skill-harness packs/
description: Create packs/noslop-skills/ in skill-harness. Package noslop quality gate workflow as a skill-harness skill — covering setup (noslop check --tier=fast pre-commit, --tier=slow pre-PR), AGENTS.md instructions, and the escape-hatch (noslop-approved label) pattern. Pull content from noslop AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md templates in the noslop repo. Include a README.md following existing pack conventions.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add specgraph-skills pack to skill-harness packs/
description: Copy and update the specgraph-skills plugin from agent-docs (plugins/specgraph-skills/) into skill-harness packs/specgraph-skills/. Ensure skills reflect latest agent-docs v0.5.1 overhaul — updated frontmatter conventions, evidence levels E0-E4, SubjectId format, and any new CLI commands. Pack should follow existing skill-harness pack structure (packs/<name>/skills/<skill>/SKILL.md + README.md).
close_reason: Closed

title: Document generated HTML opener workflow
description: Document the generated HTML review workflow after UML-first artifacts: Browser-friendly local serving, open-artifact-review --json, and the check/open commands agents should use before handoff.
close_reason: Documented host-aware generated HTML review opening, JSON opener output, and local HTTP fallback for app browsers; regenerated model review artifacts.

title: Evaluate specgraph model impact indexing
description: Plan and prototype optional agent-docs/specgraph support for model nodes and impact edges so changed files and specs can resolve to impacted canonical model artifacts.
close_reason: Added model-impact prototype that maps changed files to impacted model artifacts from manifest touchpoints.

title: Add browser preview diagnostics for developer artifacts
description: Document and wire local browser-preview diagnostics for generated developer artifacts: how to generate review HTML, where to find it, why it may be missing, and how desktop agents should open or surface it.
close_reason: Added generated-project review opener script, package scripts, setup-proof wiring, docs, and tests for best-surface HTML artifact opening across Codex app, Claude desktop, CLI, and headless contexts.

title: Add model metadata touchpoint contract
description: Define and scaffold per-model metadata fields for stable modelId, implementationTouchpoints, docTouchpoints, evidenceLinks, reviewRequired, updateTriggers, and driftVerdict output.
close_reason: Implemented model metadata inventory checks covering owner, source, review surface, evidence, and implementation/doc touchpoints; artifact checks now gate it.

title: Plan model-to-code human artifact workflow
description: Create a source-backed planning artifact and generated browser review surface for a skill-harness/agent-docs approach to canonical model-to-code mapping, Markdown human documentation, drift gates, and browser-friendly human artifacts.
notes: Follow-up implementation in this session: enriched generated model-review HTML into a tabbed developer artifact with overview, visuals, source, evidence, diff/comparison panels, static diagram previews, and embedded safe screenshot/image evidence.
close_reason: Produced source-backed planning artifact, generated browser review surface, manifest entry, and follow-up delivery issues.

title: Add computed suite graph and repo drift check
description: Implement a repo-local computed suite graph over scripts/dependencies.json, scripts/agent_loadouts.json, agent templates, and embedded pack metadata, then add generated docs and a deterministic drift check that does not depend on user-home state.
close_reason: Suite drift check, generated suite docs, and model impact reporting are wired into the repo workflow and validated.

title: Add architecture/model drift gates to CI
description: Add deterministic CI gates for Go tests/build, Python script syntax, suite drift checks, temp-home render smoke, and temp-repo setup-project artifact checks. Keep environment checks separate from repo drift checks.
close_reason: CI artifact gate is now read-only and runs model artifact policy, inventory, generated review drift, manifest, and HTML checks; Go, Python, suite drift, and artifact gates passed.

title: Self-model skill-harness developer artifacts
description: Add source-backed model inventory for skill-harness itself, including system context, domain language, use-case index, setup-project flow, artifact manifest linkage, and source/generated boundary rules for generated/ and dist/.
close_reason: Added repo self-model inventory, six canonical model sources, manifest metadata, generated HTML review surfaces, and policy gates.

title: Research canonical model-to-code documentation alignment
description: Discovery task for external and internal research on keeping canonical UML/C4/model artifacts mapped to code and generated Markdown/human documentation in skill-harness.
close_reason: External/internal discovery completed; follow-up implementation issues filed.

title: Add canonical model inventory scaffold
description: Scaffold a default source-backed model inventory for UML-first projects: system context, domain language, use-case index, ownership/freshness metadata, and a feature-change update matrix mapping workflow/domain/boundary changes to model kinds.
close_reason: Added the canonical model inventory scaffold and explicit agent/doc rules for keeping it aligned with manifest and model sources.

title: Add model-review HTML generator
description: Build safe static HTML human review surfaces for model-view and model-diff artifacts under generated/review/models, including source/evidence links, freshness state, before/after comparison layouts, local navigation, and accessibility structure.
close_reason: Added safe static model-review HTML generator, model review scripts, package script wiring, and generator/html policy tests.

title: Make UML-first artifacts the default
description: Review and plan the shift from optional --enable-modeling to UML-first developer artifacts by default, including human HTML review surfaces, continuous system understanding, agent update rules, generated review policy, and compatibility with existing artifact profiles.

title: Fix agent-loop Beads skip metadata
description: When setup-project uses the agent-loop profile with --skip-beads, generated project metadata should not claim Beads is the default issue tool. Pass Beads state into the scaffold or emit an explicit-request fallback.
close_reason: Fixed: agent-loop scaffold now records explicit-human-request when Beads is skipped; policy checker and smoke test cover the fallback.

title: Add first-class modeling artifact scaffold
description: Implement the proposed source-first UML/UWE modeling specialization for developer artifacts: setup flag, model directories, model-diff template, manifest metadata, model policy checker, package scripts, setup-proof wiring, and scaffold tests.
close_reason: Implemented: setup flag, model directories, model-diff template, model policy checker, package scripts, setup-proof wiring, tests, and generated-project smoke coverage are in place.

title: Research UML-backed developer artifact workflow
description: Investigate how skill-harness should support canonical UML/UWE-style model artifacts, generated review surfaces, before/after model diffs, and agent-updated model workflows for target repos such as macquariecollege.
close_reason: Research artifact created and follow-up implementation issue filed as skill-harness-5ab

title: Add self-improving agent loop profile
description: Implement a concrete skill-harness capability for AI-native self-improving agent loops: document the architecture, add installable skill guidance, wire it into dependencies/loadouts, and verify docs/tests stay coherent.
design: Add an embedded agent-operating-skills pack for general frontier-agent operating loops, not product-specific control-plane workflows. Wire it into architect/workflow/delivery/review/security/research loadouts and finish setup-project's agent-loop artifact profile with policy checks.
close_reason: Implemented agent-operating-skills pack, agent-loop project scaffold, loadout/dependency/docs wiring, and validation coverage.

title: Add integration harness for setup-project and pack wiring
description: Add fixture-based integration tests with temp homes/repos for setup-project profiles, embedded pack install, render/check wiring, and media artifact scaffold behavior.
close_reason: Implemented subprocess-based CLI integration tests for setup-project media scaffolding, selected embedded pack install, agents-only render/check wiring, and explicit render/check command forwarding. V&V passed: issue-specific CLI tests, go test ./cmd/skill-harness, go test ./..., npm run artifacts:check, Python py_compile, JSON integrity, suite drift check, go build.

title: Add install-proof artifact for setup-project
description: Emit a machine-readable setup proof recording selected profile, package manager, installed tools, Beads status, noslop/agent-docs checks, artifact policy checks, rendered paths, and skipped capabilities.
close_reason: Implemented setup-proof artifact for setup-project and documented the governed agent-loop profile with passing Go and dependency checks.

title: Define third-party pack manifest and lockfile schema
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: public skill ecosystems now expect provenance, compatibility, version pinning, and update checks. Define a lean skill-harness manifest and lockfile schema that can be generated from reviewed first-party and third-party packs without turning skill-harness into a broad marketplace. Include source URL/ref/subpath, trust tier, license, target agents, install scope, allowed assets, executable/MCP requirements, content hashes, and validation status.

title: Whole-repo review with external research
description: Review the skill-harness repository end to end with specialist agent perspectives and external research. Cover architecture, security, quality, workflow, generated artifacts, setup-project behavior, embedded packs, and follow-up risks. No code edits intended unless explicitly requested after review.
close_reason: Whole-repo review completed with architecture, security, quality, workflow, and external research findings.

title: Add source-backed model artifact scaffolding
description: Add provenance/freshness metadata and model-diagram guidance for developer artifacts so generated HTML review surfaces can safely include source-backed Mermaid/C4/UML-style diagrams without becoming canonical truth.
close_reason: Implemented source-backed model artifact scaffold, manifest policy checker, model-review skill, docs, loadout wiring, and verification tests.

title: Decide first skill-harness opportunity slice
description: Review generated opportunity artifact and choose the first implementation slice: capability profiles/catalog, demo-production media profile, install-proof artifact, or CI/integration harness.
notes: Implemented lean first slice: external intake now detects plugin/Codex/MCP surfaces, licenses, lock/index files, executable helpers, blocked/review flags, trust recommendations, JSON output, and fail-on-blocked behavior. Verified with py_compile and fixture scan.
close_reason: Implemented the first lean opportunity slice: external skill intake now reports provenance/risk/compatibility signals, emits JSON, and can fail on blocked patterns. Follow-up schema work filed separately.

title: Brainstorm skill-harness opportunity artifact
description: Research 45ck repos and synthesize candidate skill-harness additions into a generated review artifact for user review.
close_reason: Generated and reviewed skill-harness opportunity artifact source plus local HTML review surface.

title: Research 45ck public-facing skill-harness opportunities
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: inspect public 45ck GitHub organization repositories, identify cross-repo patterns and suite gaps, and propose high-leverage additions for skill-harness with evidence from repo names, descriptions, and topics. No file edits required.
close_reason: Research was consolidated into skill-harness opportunity review artifact and follow-up decision issue skill-harness-fg1.

title: Document developer artifact capability
description: Add internal skill-harness documentation for the developer artifact scaffold, profiles, generated HTML policy, setup-project behavior, and maintenance rules.
close_reason: Added internal developer artifact documentation and linked it from repo entrypoints.

title: Security review: generated HTML developer artifacts in setup-project
description: Review the security, privacy, and operational risks of generated HTML developer artifacts in the skill-harness setup-project flow. Cover self-contained HTML, inline JS/CSS, local file previews, Claude/Codex desktop previews, browser plugins, external assets, secrets leakage, prompt injection, CSP/sandboxing, git diffs, generated artifacts, and team sharing. Output concrete guardrails and tests/linters to enforce. No file edits in this task.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design optional developer-artifact capability architecture
description: Produce an implementation proposal for an optional developer-artifact capability in skill-harness. Cover CLI flags, config shape, pack/skill list, setup-project scaffold, agent loadout changes, migration path, risks, and acceptance criteria. No file edits in this session.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement optional developer artifact scaffolding
description: Add a developer-artifact-skills embedded pack and default-but-skippable setup-project scaffolding. Include --skip-artifacts and --artifact-profile auto|markdown|html|dual|none, update loadouts/dependencies, and document canonical Markdown/TOON/specgraph source with generated HTML review surfaces.
close_reason: Implemented developer artifact scaffold, embedded skill pack, profile config, docs, tests, and HTML policy checker.

title: Plan optional developer artifact scaffolding for setup-project
description: Explore how skill-harness should add default-but-skippable developer artifact scaffolding, including Markdown/HTML artifact policy and environment-aware guidance for desktop app versus CLI/TUI agents.
close_reason: Planning pass complete; implementation follow-up filed.

title: Write cumulative analysis report after experiments 3-6 complete
description: After experiments 3, 4, 5, 6 all have results, write a cumulative analysis section to RESULTS.md covering: (1) does the gap hold at larger scale (exp 3)? (2) is the paper trail useful for maintenance (exp 4)? (3) which component drives the value (exp 5, if run)? (4) does it work non-greenfield (exp 6, if run)? Conclude with a verdict on whether the toolkit is worth the overhead for different project types.
close_reason: Closed

title: Experiment 6: non-greenfield additive (clone existing project, add feature)
description: Clone a small open-source Node.js project (e.g. a simple express todo API from GitHub). Have Group A install agent-docs on it and add a feature (user auth). Have Group B add the same feature without tools. Tests whether agent-docs works in a non-greenfield context — the user's concern that it may be greenfield-only. Score on same 7 metrics.

title: Experiment 5: toolkit component isolation (noslop-only vs specgraph-only vs full)
description: Run the auth module task with 3 groups: A=full toolkit, B=noslop-only (AGENTS.md with noslop rules, no specgraph), C=specgraph-only (spec-first workflow, no noslop), D=baseline. Identifies which component is driving the gap. Requires 4 parallel sessions. Hypothesis: specgraph drives spec/evidence metrics; noslop drives quality gate adherence; neither drives functional correctness.

title: Create experiments/ scaffold for toolkit vs baseline benchmark
description: Create experiments/ directory in skill-harness with: methodology.md (scoring rubric, metrics: spec compliance, drift, over-engineering, output quality), session-harness setup (two project dirs — group-a-with-toolkit/ and group-b-baseline/), run-session.sh script, and RESULTS.md template. Group A = agent-docs + specgraph skills + noslop. Group B = raw Claude, no tools. Same task prompt for both groups.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update setup-project to install specgraph + noslop as full toolkit
description: Extend skill-harness setup-project command (or AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md) so that project bootstrap installs both @45ck/agent-docs (runs specgraph init) and @45ck/noslop as the standard full toolkit. Document the combined setup flow. This makes skill-harness the single entry point for the full development environment.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix agent operating whitespace gate
description: Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the post-implementation review found git diff --check failures from extra blank lines at EOF in the new agent operating docs and skill files. Trim those files so the committed range passes whitespace checks.
close_reason: Trimmed agent operating EOF whitespace and restored green build/scaffold checks for current branch changes.

title: Add offline diagram renderer for model artifacts
description: Add an optional offline rendering path for source-backed model artifacts, likely using Mermaid CLI first, so Markdown/TOON/specgraph diagram sources can produce pre-rendered inline SVG for generated HTML review surfaces without browser diagram runtimes.

title: Prototype architecture-space review dashboard
description: Prototype a static generated review dashboard that combines context/container views, decision links, quality evidence, ownership, and blast-radius information from canonical artifacts and the artifact manifest.
